behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index." Slot B: model-output boundary (real embedding model output handed to index schema). Slot C: if lowered to System, a mocked/fixed embedding response can hide a real failure where the provider/model version returns a different vector length than expected. Final label: Agentic, because the decisive signal is model-output dependence (Slot B), and lowering one level misses real model-output dimension drift (Slot C). Rejection gate: Workflow was considered and rejected because this behavior checks a single agentic decision point (embedding output shape), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the API against the real embedding model configuration, capture returned vector length for representative queries, and assert exact equality to the live vector-index dimension contract.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score." Slot B: real-infra boundary (actual vector database query behavior and scoring/order semantics). Slot C: if lowered to Integration, an in-process fake repository can always return pre-sorted rows and miss real DB/index misconfiguration (wrong metric, sort direction, tie behavior). Final label: System, because the decisive signal is real-infra behavior in the vector DB engine (Slot B), and lowering misses real engine ordering faults (Slot C). Rejection gate: Agentic was considered and rejected because the behavior text is deterministic numeric ordering by cosine score, not model-judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a real vector database index with known vectors, issue a real similarity-search call through the service, and assert returned scores are monotonically non-increasing and IDs match expected cosine ordering.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "LLM re-ranking step changes the order ... when the query has nuanced intent." Slot B: model-output boundary (correctness depends on real LLM ranking judgment). Slot C: if lowered to System, a stubbed reranker can force a reorder and pass while hiding failures where the real LLM does not understand nuanced intent and leaves poor ordering unchanged. Final label: Agentic, because the decisive signal is dependence on real LLM decisions (Slot B), and lowering misses true ranking-quality failures (Slot C). Rejection gate: Workflow was considered and rejected because this behavior targets one agentic step (reranking), not a multi-agentic chain assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Use a curated nuanced-intent query set, run real LLM reranking over raw top-K candidates, and assert at least one meaningful position change occurs versus raw similarity order for designated cases.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "query with no close matches ... returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise." Slot B: model-output boundary at embedding->retrieval thresholding (semantic embedding output determines closeness). Slot C: if lowered to System, fixed/mock embeddings can hide a real failure where the production embedding model maps unrelated queries near indexed items, causing noisy false positives. Final label: Agentic, because the decisive signal is dependence on real embedding-model output content (Slot B), and lowering misses semantic false-positive behavior (Slot C). Rejection gate: Workflow was considered and rejected because this behavior can be validated at the single agentic embedding stage plus retrieval threshold, without requiring multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real embedding generation and real vector search for known out-of-domain queries, assert all returned candidates fall below the production relevance threshold and API response is an empty list.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "end-to-end latency ... to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case." Slot B: multi-step agentic boundary (embedding model call + vector retrieval + LLM reranking in sequence, with real infra latency). Slot C: if lowered to Agentic, measuring only one model step misses cumulative median latency regressions from cross-step orchestration, network hops, and queueing between stages. Final label: Workflow, because the decisive signal is sequential multi-agentic pipeline timing (Slot B), and lowering misses compounded pipeline latency failures (Slot C). Rejection gate: System was considered and rejected because behavior text explicitly requires full re-ranked end-to-end path, which includes multiple AI/ML steps rather than a single infra interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like load slice through the full API pipeline with real embedding service, real vector DB, and real LLM reranker; collect end-to-end timings and assert p50 latency < 2.0s over the sample window.
